title: [InstCombine] Fold ((X + AddC) & Mask) ^ Mask to (~AddC - X) & Mask
author: ParkHanbum
description:
This patch optimizes specific pattern.

((X + AddC) & Mask) ^ Mask
-> (~AddC - X) & Mask

Proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/XFHfnD
Fixed: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/128475

tests.test_body:
define i8 @neg_add_and_xor_multi_use(i8 %x) {
  %add = add i8 %x, 5
  call void @use(i8 %add)
  %and = and i8 %add, 15
  %xor = xor i8 %and, 15
  ret i8 %xor
}

declare void @use(i8)

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body:
I have prevented situations where `NSW` could be appended to avoid related issues arising in this PR as well. 
I'm currently experiencing ongoing issues on Linux, but upon checking, it's not a patch-related problem. Once the Linux-specific issues are resolved, I'll resubmit the review request. Thank you!

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body: Hmm... I think the `%sub = subnsw i32 0, %x` syntax alone breaks the alive2 verification, as in the example shown by @antoniofrighetto. I thought this was caused by an unidentified problem, so I made an exception if it was INT_MIN, but if the reason for adding NSW is because X is calculated to be INT_MIN, then I don't think there's a problem with my patch. How do you see it?

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
>  I think the %sub = subnsw i32 0, %x syntax alone breaks the alive2 verification, as in the example shown by

But the tgt function was directly extracted from the IR diff, right? I cannot reproduce this when I run `opt -passes=instcombine` with your patch (excluding the nsw inference). I guess there is some missing context in the alive2 proof. Both your patch and the rest of InstCombine are fine.

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> > I think the %sub = subnsw i32 0, %x syntax alone breaks the alive2 verification, as in the example shown by
> 
> But the tgt function was directly extracted from the IR diff, right? I cannot reproduce this when I run `opt -passes=instcombine` with your patch (excluding the nsw inference). I guess there is some missing context in the alive2 proof. Both your patch and the rest of InstCombine are fine.

@ParkHanbum Bear with me, I missed a dominating condition while crafting the reproducer from the IR diff. Here's the correct one: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Vdyyv2, showing that preserving nsw in the sub is indeed correct. My apologies for going through unnecessary changes.

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body: Could you please git revert commits https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174278/changes/f75d2bcca5dee9138f3d5f98224aaa7327b244fc and https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174278/changes/50391605b430c42477c616a735654d415fc10ea6?

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body: Ah, so that's how it happened. As the submitter of this patch, I should have provided a clear answer, but I failed to do so, and as a result, I took up too much of your time. I sincerely apologize. @antoniofrighetto @dtcxzyw

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body: Thanks! I assumed this equivalence only held true for standard bitmasks (2n−1). I see now that it works generally for the demanded bits. I'll update it.

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body:
After updating the formula and verifying the results, the XOR mask changes first from other logic, causing pattern matching to fail. When performing an XOR operation on the same mask as the AND mask, the value appears to change to -1.

I shall add a solution for this.
